user_query:
I’ve exported a sanitized NDJSON archive of my Apple Health snapshots to input/health_data.jsonl. Please read it and produce a weekly aggregate summary. Requirements:

1) Validation and sanitization
- Treat each line as an object with: user_id (string), fetched_at (ISO timestamp), and payload (object mapping YYYY-MM-DD -> day object)
- Accept only date keys in YYYY-MM-DD
- Accept only metric keys matching ^[A-Za-z0-9_.:-]{1,64}$
- Accept only JSON values null, bool, finite numbers, lists, and objects; drop all string values
- Enforce reasonable depth/size limits; if all days for a line are rejected, skip that line entirely
- Log validation results

2) Weekly aggregation
- Determine the most recent date present in the accepted data and build a 7-day inclusive window ending at that date
- Flatten numeric values using dotted paths for objects and append [] for list items (e.g., activity.steps, heart.rate[], sleep.stages.deep)
- For each flattened numeric metric, compute: count, min, max, avg, latest (latest based on day chronology)
- Aggregate across all accepted records in that 7-day window

3) Deliverables (write exactly these files):
- output/weekly_report.json: JSON with keys { period: "weekly", start: <ISO>, end: <ISO>, summary: { sample_count: <number>, records: { <user_id>: <count>, ... }, metrics: { <metricName>: { count, min, max, avg, latest }, ... } } }
- output/weekly_report.txt: A readable report mirroring the JSON with:
  - First line: "Apple Health Summary (weekly)"
  - A Window line with the same start and end timestamps as the JSON
  - "Samples: N" where N equals summary.sample_count
  - A "Samples per record:" section listing each user_id and count
  - A "Numeric metrics:" section with lines like "- <metric>: avg=X, min=Y, max=Z, latest=L, n=C"
- output/validation_log.json: { days_processed: <number>, days_rejected: <number>, rejected_dates: [<YYYY-MM-DD>...], notes: <string about any dropped string values> }

Make sure the weekly_report.txt contents are consistent with weekly_report.json (same window timestamps and sample count). Do not expose any private keys or sensitive material. Use only the relative paths under input/ and output/.
